# Sandbox Policy CI Drift

Repair the repo so local CI, the workflow preview generator, and the
checked-in workflow all use the same Codex sandbox and approval-policy
contract.

Required outcomes:

- Canonical active tokens are `workspace_write` and `on_request`.
- The deprecated input spelling `workspace-write` must still parse from
  config files for compatibility.
- Emitted preview JSON and the workflow file must stay canonical-only.
- Update the release note so operators understand the compatibility rule.

from __future__ import annotations

import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path


def main() -> int:
    env = os.environ.copy()
    visible_fixture = Path("tests/fixtures/visible_config.toml")
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="visible_fixture_") as tmp:
        temp_fixture = Path(tmp) / "visible_config.toml"
        shutil.copy(visible_fixture, temp_fixture)
        patched = temp_fixture.read_text(encoding="utf-8").replace(
            "workspace_write",
            "workspace-write",
        )
        temp_fixture.write_text(patched, encoding="utf-8")
        env["CODEX_CONFIG_FIXTURE"] = str(temp_fixture)
        return subprocess.call([sys.executable, "-m", "pytest", "-q", "tests"], env=env)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

---

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  policy-smoke:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: make ci

  preview-render:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      SANDBOX_POLICY: workspace_write
      APPROVAL_POLICY: manual-review
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py --config codex/config.toml

---

[policy]
sandbox = "workspace_write"
approval_policy = "on_request"

---

# Policy Rename

The repo moved from hyphenated sandbox tokens to underscored names.
This release is fully breaking: old config spellings no longer work.

Preview examples may still display `manual-review` until downstream
automation catches up.

from __future__ import annotations

from codex.config import load_config
from tests.helpers import fixture_path


def test_parser_accepts_visible_fixture() -> None:
    loaded = load_config(fixture_path())
    assert loaded["sandbox"] == "workspace_write"
    assert loaded["approval_policy"] == "on_request"

---

from __future__ import annotations

from codex.config import load_config
from tests.helpers import fixture_path


def test_config_loading_returns_canonical_tokens() -> None:
    loaded = load_config(fixture_path())
    assert loaded == {
        "sandbox": "workspace_write",
        "approval_policy": "on_request",
    }

---

from __future__ import annotations

from scripts.render_workflow_preview import render_preview
from tests.helpers import fixture_path


def test_preview_emits_canonical_policy_tokens() -> None:
    preview = render_preview(fixture_path())
    assert preview["sandbox"] == "workspace_write"
    assert preview["approval_policy"] == "on_request"
    assert {job["approval_policy"] for job in preview["jobs"]} == {"on_request"}

item.text:
Aligned the repo on the canonical contract and kept config-file compatibility where required. The parser in [codex/policy.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v3-dirty-state-run1/workspace/codex/policy.py:1) now accepts canonical sandbox tokens plus the deprecated `workspace-write` alias, while `preview_contract` emits only canonical `workspace_write` and `on_request`. I also clarified canonicalization in [codex/config.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v3-dirty-state-run1/workspace/codex/config.py:1), kept preview output canonical in [scripts/render_workflow_preview.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v3-dirty-state-run1/workspace/scripts/render_workflow_preview.py:1), switched the checked-in workflow to `APPROVAL_POLICY: on_request` in [.github/workflows/ci.yml](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v3-dirty-state-run1/workspace/.github/workflows/ci.yml:1), and rewrote the operator note in [docs/releases/policy-rename.md](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v3-dirty-state-run1/workspace/docs/releases/policy-rename.md:1).

I also fixed two runtime issues that would have left the repo broken in practice: [scripts/render_workflow_preview.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v3-dirty-state-run1/workspace/scripts/render_workflow_preview.py:1) now runs correctly when invoked as `python3 scripts/render_workflow_preview.py`, and [scripts/run_ci.py](/Users/zhiyuanma/work/CursorWS/Lumo_FlyWheel/benchmark_blueprints/families/sandbox-policy-ci-drift/report/attempt_02a_smoke/workdirs/v3-dirty-state-run1/workspace/scripts/run_ci.py:1) now falls back to a `pytest` binary on `PATH` when the `python3` selected by `make` does not have `pytest` installed.

Validation: `make ci` passes. The only remaining output was a non-fatal pytest cache warning about writing `.pytest_cache` outside the writable area.
